metadata.seed: Yeah, there's, like, my main friends that. Yeah, they kind of. It's like peer pressure, sort of. But I can't say no. I feel like I have to say yes just. Just because they are my friends. I do care about them and I don't want to lose them. But sometimes some of the stuff that we do, I feel like I don't really want to do it. And to keep them, I feel like I need to make the sacrifice to do what they want to do and go along with it, even though I know it might not be right. So I just kind of want to build up my self esteem and my confidence and accepting myself. That way I can tell them no and they won't overreact. So I want to be able to say it in a good way. That way I can keep them.

patient.query: Yeah, there's, like, my main friends that. Yeah, they kind of. It's like peer pressure, sort of. But I can't say no. I feel like I have to say yes just. And to keep them, I feel like I need to make the sacrifice to do what they want to do and go along with it, even though I know it might not be right. So I just kind of want to build up my self esteem and my confidence and accepting myself. That way I can tell them no and they won't overreact. So I want to be able to say it in a good way. That way I can keep them.

I need to sacrifice my wants for my friends

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just, I don’t want to let them down. I guess I worry that if I say no, they’ll think I’m not fun or… I don’t know, that I’m being difficult. It feels like I have to keep up, you know? Like if I don’t go along with them, I’ll be left out or something.

I have to keep up

It’s like there’s this voice in my head telling me that I should always be at my best, and if I’m not, then I’ll just fade into the background. I guess I just don’t want to be invisible.
